mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly unemployment rate rises above 7.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE > 7.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month simultaneously
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-declining instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declines below 105.0 signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, constraining earnings growth and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 58% of instances when INDPRO < 105.0, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100,000 net monthly additions while unemployment rate remains stable, signals labor market slack that reduces wage pressure and inflation expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 100,000 and UNRATE unchanged or rising
regime_hint: labor market deceleration with stable unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predictive model.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) rises above 220.0 while federal funds rate remains below 2.0% signals real rate compression that erodes fixed-income returns and redirects capital to equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 52% of instances meeting both conditions, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 750,000 units annualized signals residential construction weakness that reduces construction employment and building material demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when HOUST < 750,000, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 106.5 while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously grows above 150,000 monthly additions signals synchronized supply and labor expansion that supports earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 106.5 and PAYEMS month-over-month change > 150,000 in same month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.
